programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED - Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Intermediate First Aid (CSA Z1210:24).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Standard First Aid (CPR C) Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Lifesaving Society Standard First Aid with CPR-C (only current certifications from the Lifesaving Society will be accepted).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED - Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Intermediate First Aid (CSA Z1210:24).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Standard First Aid (CPR C) Recert

Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Lifesaving Society Standard First Aid with CPR-C (only current certifications from the Lifesaving Society will be accepted).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Intermediate First Aid with CPR-C & AED - Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society (in accordance with CSA Z1210:24). This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Intermediate First Aid (CSA Z1210:24).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Learn comprehensive first aid training covering spinal injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the proper use of automated external defibrillators.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Standard First Aid (CPR C) Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course provides a review of first aid and CPR skills. Practical skills are assessed through scenarios and knowledge will be tested through a written evaluation.Prerequisite: Current Lifesaving Society Standard First Aid with CPR-C (only current certifications from the Lifesaving Society will be accepted).For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered with the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Arthritis
programs.description: Enjoy a work out in the water to improve your fitness and reduce stress on your joints. Designed for participants with arthritis and joint injuries, movements will focus on improving range of motion through muscle conditioning, flexibility and strength-building exercises.It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.
